compiled_state.title: RED: Tests asserting feature-intent detection guidance in using-lockpick
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Test file exists at tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py
  Verify: test -f tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py
- [ ] Test asserts SKILL.md contains a Feature Intent Detection section
  Verify: grep -q "feature.*intent\|Feature.*Intent" tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py
- [ ] Test asserts explicit signal patterns are present (new feature, create an epic, I want to build)
  Verify: grep -q "new feature\|create an epic\|I want to build" tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py
- [ ] Test asserts HOOK-INJECTION.md contains matching guidance
  Verify: grep -q "HOOK-INJECTION\|hook.injection" tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py
- [ ] All new tests FAIL (RED) against current unmodified files
  Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py --tb=no -q 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED\|failed"
- [ ] .test-index entries added for both source files
  Verify: grep -q "test_using_lockpick_feature_intent" .test-index

## TDD Requirement
Write failing tests that read SKILL.md and HOOK-INJECTION.md content and assert the feature-intent detection section exists with concrete signal patterns. Tests must be behavioral — they read the file content and check for structural sections, not grep source code. Tests should FAIL against current files (no feature-intent section exists yet).

## File Impact
- tests/skills/test_using_lockpick_feature_intent.py (create)
- .test-index (modify — add mappings)
